mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, increasing present values of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.02 during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering immediate negative equity price adjustments.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that drives corporate revenue forecasts upward, producing sustained positive equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in over 60% of periods where both retail sales and housing starts rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a high-inflation environment signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of observations when industrial production declines during high inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 2.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and forces immediate negative repricing of equity assets over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 65% of months where CPI exceeds the 2.5 percent annualized threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in more than 55% of periods where payrolls rise and unemployment falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth deceleration reduces aggregate demand expectations, lowering corporate earnings projections and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month as investors price in slower growth.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in over 60% of quarters where GDP growth shows sequential deceleration.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in industrial production during a low-rate environment signals robust manufacturing output that improves corporate balance sheets, triggering positive equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of observations when industrial production rises during low-rate periods.
